title: Forensic Imaging & Hash Verification Workflow
description: Explain the forensic imaging procedure — Original Drive, Write-Blocker, Forensic Workstation, Forensic Image, and Hash Verification — and verify image integrity by comparing MD5 or SHA-256 hashes. The Simulate Tamper control changes one byte of the image and recomputes its hash, showing how the entire value changes and the comparison flips from MATCH to MISMATCH.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Digital Forensics
Evidence Integrity
Hashing
Chain of Custody
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — explain the imaging workflow and how hash comparison verifies integrity.

educational.topic: Forensic disk imaging and verifying a copy with cryptographic hashes
learningObjectives: Explain the five stages of forensic imaging and the role of the write-blocker.
Explain how comparing a hash of the original to a hash of the image proves the copy is identical.
Describe why a single changed byte produces a completely different hash, so any tampering is detected.
educational.prerequisites: Basic idea of a file and of copying data; no cryptography background required

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Horizontal five-station workflow with animated data-flow dots and a write-blocker that blocks reverse flow. Clicking a station reveals its purpose and procedure. A hash-comparison panel shows the original and image hashes side by side in the selected algorithm; the Simulate Tamper button changes the image and recomputes its hash to flip MATCH into MISMATCH.
pedagogical.assessment: Students read each station's role and observe that an untampered image yields matching hashes (MATCH); after Simulate Tamper the image hash changes entirely and the result becomes MISMATCH, illustrating integrity verification
pedagogical.modelType: Workflow diagram plus a hash-comparison panel backed by correctly formatted example hash values (32 hex MD5, 64 hex SHA-256) for the untampered and tampered states
controls: Click a station to reveal its purpose and procedure
